title: Sailing Weather App with Interactive Map, Weather Animations, and Direction Recommendations
description: A full-featured sailing weather application that shows an interactive map with the user's boat, animated weather overlays (wind, rain, pressure), and a directional compass rose that recommends or warns about heading choices based on weather conditions ahead. Uses Open-Meteo's free API for marine/weather data and Leaflet for mapping.

plan.summary:
Build a sailing weather app with an interactive Leaflet map centered on the user's boat position, animated weather visualizations (wind particles, rain, pressure gradients), and a direction recommendation compass rose. The app fetches weather data from Open-Meteo's free API (no key needed) for current conditions and multi-hour forecasts. For direction analysis, it samples weather at points ~30nm in each of 8 cardinal directions and scores them by wind favorability, wave conditions, and precipitation.

All units are fixed: nautical conventions (knots for wind, nautical miles for distance) and metric (°C for temperature, hPa for pressure, meters for wave height). No user settings, no unit conversion layer — keeps the codebase simple and focused.

Technical approach: Leaflet.js for the map (free, tile-based), Open-Meteo API for weather + marine data (free, no API key), Canvas overlays for particle animations, CSS animations for the boat, server-side weather fetching to avoid CORS and rate limits. Single-page design with the map as the hero element and overlay panels for conditions and compass rose.

steps.description: Step 1 — Configs: Set default configs DEFAULT_FORECAST_HOURS=48, DIRECTION_SAMPLE_DISTANCE_NM=30, COMPASS_DIRECTIONS=8. No database tables needed — all data comes from the weather API in real-time.

Step 2 — Weather fetch function: `fetchWeather` — calls Open-Meteo API with lat/lon, returns hourly forecast data including wind speed (converted to knots), wind direction, gusts, temperature (°C), pressure (hPa), precipitation (mm), cloud cover, and marine data (wave height in meters, wave direction, wave period). Open-Meteo returns m/s for wind and km/h options — we convert to knots server-side (×1.94384 from m/s).

Step 3 — Direction analysis function: `analyzeDirections` — takes a position and calculates 8 points at ~30nm in each cardinal/intercardinal direction. Fetches weather for each point, scores conditions (wind speed vs. comfort range, wind angle relative to heading, wave height, precipitation). Returns scored directions with color ratings: green (favorable), yellow (caution), red (dangerous). Includes text summaries like 'Strong headwinds, 2m seas'.

Step 4 — Weather API handler: `getWeather` (GET /api/weather?lat=&lon=) fetches and returns weather data for a position. Includes current conditions + hourly forecast. All values in nautical/metric units.
Step 5 — Direction analysis API handler: `getDirections` (GET /api/directions?lat=&lon=) returns the 8-direction analysis with scores and recommendations.

Step 6 — Main CSS asset: Full app styling — dark nautical theme, map fills viewport, translucent overlay panels for conditions and compass rose, compass rose with color-coded segments, responsive layout, animated boat, timeline slider styling.

Step 7 — Client-side JavaScript asset: Leaflet map initialization, boat SVG marker with rotation, Canvas weather animation overlay (wind particles flowing in wind direction, rain particles, pressure gradient), compass rose component with interactive direction display, forecast timeline slider, geolocation for initial position, fetch calls to API endpoints. Wind shown as arrow particles flowing with speed-proportional velocity; rain as blue dots; pressure as subtle color gradient. Units displayed as kn, °C, hPa, m, nm throughout.

Step 8 — Main page template: HTML shell with Leaflet CSS/JS CDN links, app CSS/JS asset references, map container div, overlay panel containers (current conditions, compass rose, timeline), boat info bar.
Step 9 — Page serve handler and route registration: `serveApp` handler serves the main template. Register all routes: GET / → serveApp, GET /api/weather, GET /api/directions.

Step 10 — Tests: Test weather fetch function (knot conversion, data structure), direction analysis logic (scoring, color ratings), API handler responses, and route registration. Run tests and verify coverage.
